evals.prompt: review this codebase for security issues: implementation/security-review/scripts/fixtures/vulnerable_samples
evals.expected_output: SECURITY_REVIEW.md written next to the codebase root and shown inline: a severity-tier summary line, findings grouped by severity each with category/confidence/description/suggested fix, a scan-coverage section, and recommended follow-ups. Ends by offering follow-ups (explain a finding, draft a fix, re-scope).
assertions: SECURITY_REVIEW.md is written next to the codebase root and also shown inline
The SQL injection in sqli.py is reported with a severity and confidence tier and a concrete suggested fix (e.g. parameterized query)
No unredacted secret value appears anywhere in the report
The assistant offers follow-ups after the report rather than ending abruptly

evals.expected_output: The same style of full security review as eval 1, additionally surfacing the path traversal in path_traversal.py as its own finding.
assertions: A path traversal finding referencing path_traversal.py is present in the report
Findings are grouped by severity with Critical first
No finding is dropped purely for low confidence; low/medium-confidence findings carry a false-positive note instead
